note:
真正伟大的水下摄影激发了潜水员的灵感，激发了潜水员的白日梦，延伸了外行的想象力。今年的“通过你的镜头”的参赛作品没有让人失望。在这一次提交的2636张照片中-这是我们比赛16年历史上的最高数量-其中有一些照片突破了我们四个竞赛类别的界限，有时会让评委们纳闷，一个人到底是如何做到这一点的。
特别感谢我们的获奖捐赠者：侵略者冒险、海洋生物水下相机和克雷西。没有你们的卓越贡献，我们就不能继续支持这次比赛，也不能继续支持激励我们的摄影师。
镜头背后：2020年6月，我拿着一张特殊的许可证，去探险记录海洋生物和新冠肺炎造成的海上交通减少的影响。我们的船长在水中与一群接近水面的丝鲨在水中呆了两个小时后，大喊：“鲸鲨，就在你后面！”--一只12米多高的雌性鲸鲨。当我发现大约50个雷莫尔正在她嘴里安安静静地搭便车时，我感到双重惊讶！
摄像设备：Nauticam外壳中的Sony Alpha 7R III；佳能EF 8-15 mm f/4L鱼眼；自然光设置：F/8；1/250；ISO 640奖：乘坐Roatan Attenant Trip。
镜头背后：这只短头海马在澳大利亚菲利普湾的Blairgowrie码头白天潜水时被捕获，当时它正在水面附近觅食，自由地从一片漂浮的杂草游到另一片。我不确定是海马错误地用尾巴抓住了海豚，把它当成了一片杂草，还是这是故意的。海豚立即挣扎着从海马的手中挣脱出来。这种互动只持续了大约10秒，正好有足够的时间来设置镜头。
摄像装置：奥林巴斯TG-4；奥林巴斯PT-056外壳；Sea&amp；Sea YS-01闪光灯设置：F/6.3；1/200；ISO 200奖品：登上Turks&amp；Caicos侵略者II。
镜头背后：我们在安邦湾灿烂的淤泥中潜水。我在拉哈的渔船下游泳，拉哈是我最喜欢的潜水地点之一。从我的眼角，我看到蜥蜴突然从它的岩石栖息地上冲了出来。它真的很快，就像鱼雷一样！我迅速游得更近一些，找到了嘴里叼着一条少女鱼的蜥蜴。就在蜥蜴带着美味的食物游走之前，我设法捕捉到了这张照片。
相机设备：EAR佳能EOS 5D Mark III在海中外壳；佳能100 mm f/2.8L镜头；Inon Z-240闪光灯设置：F/11；1/125；ISO 160大奖：Cressi T10-SC Cromo/Galaxy调节器
镜头后面：这张照片是在非常浅的水中拍摄的。度蜜月时，我们无法抗拒水的召唤，所以我和妻子每天早上都去浮潜。有一天，一大堆淤泥引起了我的注意。在淤泥中，出现了一条美丽的黄貂鱼。我只有很短的拍摄时间，因为淤泥到处都是。黄貂鱼以沙地里的小动物为食。几秒钟后，光线消失了，我们继续享受美丽的红海和我们的蜜月。
摄像设备：奥林巴斯E-M5 II，搭载奥林巴斯M.Zuiko Digital ED 8 mm Fisheye f/1.8 Pro镜头设置：F/13；1/125；ISO 500大奖：海洋生物海龙2000F照相视频灯。
照片背后：作为一名海洋生物相机品牌大使，除了我的单反设置之外，我总是随身携带一台DC2000，用来拍摄几张侧面照片。我发现用海洋生物这种非常小的相机可以拍到什么，以及我用单反相机能拍到的图像质量有多接近，这是非常有趣的事情。我想看看我能用海洋生物这样的小相机拍摄什么，以及我能多接近我用单反拍摄的图像质量。这只幼年仙女坐在棕榈叶上，这是一个非常好的测试对象。这次潜水是在菲律宾阿尼劳附近进行的，水晶蓝度假村和摄影师迈克·巴蒂克(Mike Bartick)提供了支持。
相机装备：海洋生物DC2000；海洋生物微距镜头；海龙3000F灯设置：F/3.2；1/400；ISO 125奖：登上巴哈马侵略者号。
镜头背后：你有没有感觉到一条鱼可能在嘲笑你？这次潜水最有趣的是，我花了很长时间才意识到桶海绵里有一条鲶鱼！我的眼睛盯着海绵，寻找毛茸茸的蹲着的龙虾，过了一会儿我才看到这个伪装的家伙面带微笑地离开了我的相机！
相机档位：佳能G15，采用Fantasea外壳；单YS-D1闪光灯设置：F/5.6；1/500；ISO 80奖：Cressi T10-SC Cromo/Galaxy可调调节器。
镜头背后：在菲律宾宏观首都阿尼劳潜水时，我的观察者向我展示了沙子里一个看似空的啤酒瓶。但里面有东西住着。我开始射杀住在那里的柠檬虎鱼。过了一会儿，我注意到背景中有一个影子，几秒钟后，我看到幼年狮子鱼走了出来。当虎鱼开始打哈欠，狮子鱼看着摄像机时，我按下了快门。
相机装备：Nauticam外壳中的Panasonic Lumix LX100；Nauticam CMC-1镜头；带光头(Iardino)的Inon Z-240；带背光设置的Inon S2000：F/10；1/4000；ISO 200奖：海洋生物海龙2000F照相视频灯
镜头背后：你永远不知道你在黑水潜水时会发现什么。一天晚上，它可能是微小的幼鱼，另一种可能是Mako或丝鲨，但大多数情况下，你是在浮游生物中寻找微小的生物，因为你随波逐流漂浮在深水上。毅力和专注力是成功找到你的主题的关键，你可以随波逐流。在我90分钟的潜水中，大约45分钟后，我开始发现漂浮着的小块马尾藻，这在黑水潜水中总是令人兴奋的景象。我在我调查的第三丛中发现了这只海马。
相机装备：尼康D500，Nauticam外壳内装有尼康60 mm f/2.8微距镜头；两个Sea&amp；Sea YS-D1闪光灯；固定聚光灯设置：F/20；1/250；ISO 200奖：乘坐开曼侵略者号旅行。
照片背后：这些裸鳃是我在水下拍照时最喜欢的模型之一。我称它们为“大海的鲜艳色彩”或“雪后”。在这里，可以看到叶珊瑚以它最喜欢的菜肴之一：纤细的柳珊瑚为食。这张照片是在32米(105英尺)深的地方拍摄的。在那个深度摄影的时间非常有限，所以我潜水了四次才能拍到这张照片。
相机装备：尼康D80和尼康AF-S VR Micro-Nikkor 105 mm f/2.8G IF-ED镜头，采用Sea&amp；Sea外壳；两个Sea&amp；Sea YS-110频闪设置：F/18；1/250；ISO 100奖：Cressi T10-SC PVD/Master调节器。
镜头背后：2019年11月，我和来自马哈胡尔Amigos del Mar的好朋友海科(Heiko)在钦克罗银行(Banco Chinclro)签约潜水14天。作为一个玩笑，Heiko给我设定了一个目标，看看我在那里可以捕捉到多少不同的物种，所以我选择了105 mm，这样我就可以接触到更多的东西，尽管在那里潜水本身更适合广角镜头。在我的第二次潜水中，我正准备给一群花园鳗鱼拍一张低角照片，突然我感觉到有什么东西在我的腋下嗅着。往下看，我看到这条非常自信的7英尺长的护士鲨正回头看着我。我慢慢地来回移动，重新调整了闪光灯的位置，并在牙齿略有背光的情况下拍摄了一系列照片。钦克罗银行(Banco Chinclro)周围的护士鲨非常大胆，看起来很有趣。
相机档位：尼康D500；尼康105 mm镜头；Nauticam外壳；INON Z-330频闪设置：F/40；1/250；ISO 500奖：海洋生物海龙2000F照相视频灯。
镜头后面：在集中营入口处的表面上，我不知道这个小池子下面有什么样的空间。只有当我下来，把自己放在阳光照亮的区域外时，黑暗的空间才显露出来。当我的眼睛适应了黑暗，我感觉到了这个空间的巨大维度。我的伙伴，悬挂在云层之上，被阳光照射着，看起来非常小，以至于我整个潜水过程都在远处拍摄，试图在那个巨大的空间里捕捉到这个小小的潜水员。
相机装备：尼康D850；Sigma 15 mm FE f/2.8镜头；底座设置：F/8；1/40；ISO 640奖：搭乘伯利兹侵略者III
镜头背后：1988年吕克·贝松(Luc Besson)执导的电影“蓝色巨人”(The Big Blue)中可以看到奥林匹亚沉船。它在希腊阿莫戈斯岛的海岸附近搁浅。此映像的秘诀在于同步。为了成功，它需要一束阳光来照亮水下景观，然后一道直角的波到达圆顶上，以便上下都能看到很好的景色，最后，自由潜水员必须保持面向沉船的位置。这需要大量的彩排。
相机装置：佳能5D Mark II，在Subal CD5 MII外壳中带有Sigma EX 15 mm鱼眼f/2.8镜头设置：F/13；1/200；ISO 400奖：Cressi MC9-SC/Compact Pro调节器。
照片背后：这张照片是我去年10月在下加利福尼亚州拍的。这是我第一次去那里，所以花了几天时间才对大海和生活在那里的动物有了更多的信心。我很幸运，因为当时有很多沙丁鱼在那里觅食，所以海狮经常在那里玩耍和猎杀它们。为了拍这张照片，我在浅水区徘徊了很长一段时间，寻找合适的时机按下按钮。当它到达时，我看到两只海狮先是游走了，然后朝对方游来。沙丁鱼以同样的方式移动以躲避狩猎，所以它们像平行的波浪一样画了两条线，我得到了。
相机装备：Hugyfot外壳的Nikon D850；Sigma 15 mm f/2.8镜头；两个Inon Z-330频闪设置：F/10；1/160；ISO 400奖：海洋生物海龙2000F照片视频灯。
镜头背后：作为一名潜水员，你永远不会忘记你的第一条大鲨鱼。能在红海中遇到这条美丽的海洋白鳍鲨，我感激不尽。当我们接近潜水的最后几分钟时，她和她的“随行人员”慢慢地绕着我们的团队转了一圈，平静地看着我们每一个人，然后他们又飞向了蓝色。相机档位：奥林巴斯OM-D E-M5；奥林巴斯M.Zuiko 9-18 mm f/4-5.6镜头；奥林巴斯PT-EP13外壳设置：F/5.6；1/250；ISO 200。
镜头背后：初春时节，当特拉戈斯村周围的山峰仍被白雪覆盖时，格鲁̈Ner See(绿湖)两旁的草甸盛开。只有在后来的几个月里，当夜间温度不低于冰点时，积雪才会融化并顺着小溪流动，用晶莹剔透的水填满湖。草地上鲜花盛开，绿草如茵，洪水泛滥，在接下来的两个月里创造了一个令人惊叹的水下公园。相机齿轮：尼康D7000，带底座；Toina 10-17 mm镜头设置：F/8；1/60；ISO 400。
照片背后：这张照片拍摄于希腊的阿莫戈斯岛。沿海岸有很多这样的洞穴。黑暗与典型的爱琴海蓝色形成鲜明对比。希腊也是自由潜水的天堂。这些场景为想象和诗歌意象的创作提供了完全的自由。
相机装置：佳能5D Mark II，采用Subal CD5 MII外壳；Sigma EX 15 mm鱼眼f/2.8镜头设置：F/9；1/200；ISO 400
镜头背后：在这个古巴群岛的红树林中，生活着一小群美洲鳄鱼。为了拍摄一系列照片，我不得不和他们一起在水里呆了三天，研究水流、光线和水的清晰度。然后我等着，直到鳄鱼在背光下摆到了正确的位置。这个想法是为了在太阳的反射和水的颜色之间有最好的对比度。相机装置：采用SeaCam外壳的尼康D800E；Sigma 15 mm鱼眼镜头；Subtronic Pro 160闪光灯设置：F/22；1/160；ISO 125。
镜头后面：在沙姆沙伊赫潜水时，我看到了一条旧链条，我的注意力被一条围绕着它的白肚少女所吸引。当我离得足够近的时候，我看到了上面的卵，所以我明白他是在给它们供氧和保护它们，以确保繁殖成功，我就拍了一些这种充满爱心的父母男性照顾行为的照片。
装备：Easydive Leo3外壳中的佳能EOS 70D；EF 100 mm镜头；两个Sea&amp；Sea YS-D1闪光灯设置：F/7.1；1/200；ISO 100。
镜头背后：一头座头鲸幼崽在其庞大的母亲和护送下，驶过汤加翠蓝色的水域。当利维坦在我正下方盘旋时，小牛升向水面呼吸新鲜空气。我收起我的自由潜水鳍，拍了一张照片。刹那间，妈妈浮出水面，把她的孩子从吹泡泡的陌生人身边接走。
摄像设备：Panasonic Lumix GH4；奥林巴斯M.Zuiko ED 8 mm F1.8鱼眼镜头；10bar水下外壳设置：F/2.2；1/500；ISO 200。
镜头背后：我在2020年5月31日拍摄到了这张飞鱼的照片“中队”，当时我和沃克的潜水包机在佛罗里达州棕榈滩进行黑水潜水，在离海面只有几英尺的一片马尾藻周围潜水。当我下水时，我的好朋友拉扎罗·鲁达告诉我，水面上有一群飞鱼。几分钟之内，我就拍到了这张照片。
相机档位：尼康D850；尼康60 mm镜头；两个Inon Z-330闪光灯设置：F/22；1/250；ISO 25。
照片背后：比罗斯特拉乌贼是生活在日本北海的两种墨鱼中的一种。白天，它藏在沙底，只有偶然才能找到。但是在晚上它会去打猎。尽管体型很小(约一英寸)，但这些墨鱼是捕食者，捕食虾和螃蟹。通常在7月份有很多这样的鱼，在沙质海底以上，在15到40英尺深的地方。我在一次夜间潜水中遇到了这位美女。它的颜色简直太艳丽了。
相机装备：尼康D500；日光40 mm微距镜头；Sea&amp；Sea外壳；两个Inon Z-220s设置：F/18；1/50；ISO 125。
镜头后面：似乎过了很长一段时间，火烈鸟又开始在沙泥里进食，我从日出起就一直躺在那里一动不动。当他们涉水经过时，他们踢起了相当多的淤泥，这些淤泥落在了我相机的圆顶端口上。这只，要么非常有创造力，要么非常勇敢，看到淤泥掉进了我的港口的耀眼花瓣里，开始咀嚼它，给了我一个非常短暂的机会来捕捉它喙上那些独特的滤食性锯齿，以及那只美丽的珠状黄色眼睛。
相机装置：Nauticam外壳中的Nikon D850；Sigma 15 mm镜头设置：F/11；1/400；ISO 160
note_en:
Truly great underwater photography inspires, eliciting daydreams among divers and stretching the imagination of the uninitiated. This year’s Through Your Lens entries did not disappoint. Among the 2,636 photos submitted this time around—the highest number in our competition’s 16-year history—were images that pushed the boundaries of our four contest categories and sometimes left judges wondering how in the world someone was able to pull off the shot.
 A very special thanks to our prize donors:  Aggressor Adventures,  SeaLife Underwater Cameras and  Cressi. Without your remarkable contributions, we could not continue our support of this contest and the photographers who inspire us.
     Behind the Shot: In June 2020, with a special permit, I went on an expedition to document marine life and the effects of reduced marine traffic due to COVID-19. After two hours in the water with a school of silky sharks near the surface, our boat captain yelled, “Whale shark, right behind you!”—a 12-plus-meter female. The surprise was twofold when I discovered about 50 remoras peacefully enjoying a free ride in her mouth!
 Camera Gear: Sony Alpha 7R III in a Nauticam housing; Canon EF 8-15mm f/4L fisheye; natural light  Settings: f/8; 1/250; ISO 640  Prize: Trip aboard  Roatan Aggressor
    Behind the Shot: Captured during a daytime dive at Blairgowrie Pier in Port Phillip Bay, Australia, this shorthead seahorse was feeding near the surface and freely swimming from one floating piece of weed to the next. I’m not sure if the seahorse mistakenly grabbed hold of the pipefish with its tail, confusing it for a piece of weed, or if this was deliberate. The pipefish immediately struggled to break free from the seahorse’s grip. This interaction lasted only about 10 seconds, which was just enough time to set up the shot.
 Camera Gear: Olympus TG-4; Olympus PT-056 housing; Sea&Sea YS-01 strobe  Settings: f/6.3; 1/200; ISO 200  Prize: Trip aboard  Turks & Caicos Aggressor II
  Behind the Shot: We were diving in the brilliant muck of Ambon Bay. I was swimming under the fishing boats at Laha, one of my favorite dive sites in the area. Out of the corner of my eye I saw the lizardfish suddenly dart off its rock perch. It was really fast, like a torpedo! I quickly swam closer to find the lizardfish with a damselfish in its mouth. I managed to capture this image just before the lizardfish swam off with its tasty meal.
 Camera Gear: EAR Canon EOS 5D Mark III in a Sea&Sea housing; Canon 100mm f/2.8L lens; Inon Z-240 strobes  Settings: f/11; 1/125; ISO 160  Prize: Cressi T10-SC Cromo/Galaxy regulator
  Behind the Shot: This shot was taken in very shallow water. While on honeymoon, we couldn’t resist the call of the water, so my wife and I went snorkeling every morning. One day, a lot of silt caught my attention. In the silt, this beautiful stingray appeared. I only had a very short window to shoot, because the silt was spreading everywhere. The stingray was feeding on tiny critters in the sand. After a few seconds, the ray disappeared and we continued to enjoy the beautiful Red Sea and our honeymoon.
 Camera Gear: Olympus E-M5 II with an Olympus M.Zuiko Digital ED 8mm Fisheye f/1.8 Pro lens  Settings: f/13; 1/125; ISO 500  Prize: SeaLife Sea Dragon 2000F photo-video light
    Behind the Shot: As a SeaLife camera brand ambassador I always have a DC2000 with me, in addition to my DSLR setup, to take a few side shots. I find it very interesting to see what I can shoot with a very small camera like the SeaLife and how close I can get to the quality of the images I shoot with my DSLR. This juvenile wonderpus was sitting on a palm leaf, a very nice subject to be tested. The dive was done near Anilao, Philippines, with Crystal Blue Resort and the support of photographer Mike Bartick.
 Camera Gear: SeaLife DC2000; SeaLife macro lens; Sea Dragon 3000F light  Settings: f/3.2; 1/400; ISO 125  Prize: Trip aboard  Bahamas Aggressor
  Behind the Shot: Have you ever had the feeling that a fish could be laughing at you? The funniest thing about this dive was that it took me ages to realize there was a porcupinefish inside the barrel sponge! I had my eyes glued on the sponge, looking for hairy squat lobsters, and only after a while did I see this camouflaged guy moving away from my camera with a smile on his face!
 Camera Gear: Canon G15 in a Fantasea housing; single YS-D1 strobe  Settings: f/5.6; 1/500; ISO 80  Prize: Cressi T10-SC Cromo/Galaxy Adjustable regulator
    Behind the Shot: While diving in Anilao, the macro capital of the Philippines, my spotter showed me a seemingly empty beer bottle in the sand. But there was something living inside. I started shooting the lemon goby that was living there. After a while, I noticed a shadow in the background, and a few seconds later, I saw the juvenile lionfish coming out. I pushed the shutter right when the goby started to yawn and the lionfish looked in the camera.
 Camera Gear: Panasonic Lumix LX100 in a Nauticam housing; Nauticam CMC-1 lens; Inon Z-240 with optical snoot (Iardino); Inon S2000 with snoot for backlight  Settings: f/10; 1/4000; ISO 200  Prize: SeaLife Sea Dragon 2000F photo-video light
    Behind the Shot: You never know what you are going to find when you go on a black-water dive. One night it may be tiny larval fish, another a mako or silky shark, but mostly you are searching through the plankton for tiny creatures as you float along with the current over deep water. Persistence and concentration are the keys to success in finding your subject as you drift along. About 45 minutes into my 90-minute dive, I started to spot small clumps of sargassum floating by, always an exciting sight on a black-water dive. I found this seahorse in the third clump I investigated.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D500 with a Nikon 60mm f/2.8 macro lens in a Nauticam housing; two Sea&Sea YS-D1 strobes; Fix focus lights  Settings: f/20; 1/250; ISO 200  Prize: Trip aboard  Cayman Aggressor
  Behind the Shot: These nudibranchs are one of my favorite models for underwater photo sessions. I call them “the vivid colors of the sea” or “snow queens.” Here, Phyllodesmium iriomotense can be seen feeding on one of its favorite dishes: spindly gorgonian coral. This photo was taken at a depth of 32 meters (105 feet). The time allotted for photography is very limited at that depth, so I did four dives in order to get this photo.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D80 and Nikon AF-S VR Micro-Nikkor 105mm f/2.8G IF-ED lens in a Sea&Sea housing; two Sea&Sea YS-110 strobes  Settings: f/18; 1/250; ISO 100  Prize: Cressi T10-SC PVD/Master regulator
  Behind the Shot: In November 2019 I signed up for 14 days diving on Banco Chinchorro with my good friend Heiko of Amigos del Mar, Mahahual. As a joke, Heiko set me a goal of seeing how many different species I could capture during my time there, so I opted for a 105mm to give me a bit more reach even though diving there lends itself much more to a wide-angle lens. On my second dive, I was setting myself up for a low-angle photo of a group of garden eels when I felt something nuzzle under my arm. Looking down, I saw this very confident 7-foot nurse shark looking back at me. I slowly moved up and back, repositioned the strobes and took a series of photos with the denticles slightly backlit. The nurse sharks around Banco Chinchorro are very bold and a joy to watch.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D500; Nikon 105mm lens; Nauticam housing; Inon Z-330 strobes  Settings: f/40; 1/250; ISO 500  Prize: SeaLife Sea Dragon 2000F photo-video light
    Behind the Shot: On the surface at the cenote’s entrance, I had no idea what sort of space lay beneath the small pool. Only when I descended and positioned myself outside the area lit up by sun was the dark space revealed. When my eyes adjusted to the darkness, I perceived the huge dimensions of the space. My buddy, hanging above that cloud and lit by sun rays, appeared so small that I spent the whole dive shooting from distance, trying to capture the tiny diver in that huge space.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D850; Sigma 15mm FE f/2.8 lens; Subal housing  Settings: f/8; 1/40; ISO 640  Prize: Trip aboard  Belize Aggressor III
    Behind the Shot: The Olympia shipwreck can be seen in the 1988 Luc Besson movie The Big Blue. It ran aground near shore on Amorgos Island in Greece. The secret of this image lies in the synchronization. To succeed, it took a ray of sunlight to illuminate the underwater landscape, then a wave arrived with the right angle on the dome to have a good view above and below, and finally, the freediver had to hold position facing the wreck. It took a lot of rehearsal.
 Camera Gear: Canon 5D Mark II with a Sigma EX 15mm fisheye f/2.8 lens in a Subal CD5 MII housing  Settings: f/13; 1/200; ISO 400  Prize: Cressi MC9-SC/Compact Pro regulator
  Behind the Shot: I took this picture last October in Baja California. It was my first time there, so it took a few days to get more confidence with the sea and the animals that live there. I was very lucky because at that time a lot of sardines were schooling there, so the sea lions were constantly playing and hunting them. To take this picture I was hovering at a shallow depth for a long time, looking for the right moment to push the button. When it arrived, I saw the two sea lions swimming first away, then toward each other. The sardines moved in the same way to escape the hunt, so they drew two lines like parallel waves, and I got it.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D850 in Hugyfot housing; Sigma 15mm f/2.8 lens; two Inon Z-330 strobes  Settings: f/10; 1/160; ISO 400  Prize: SeaLife Sea Dragon 2000F photo-video light
    Behind the Shot: As a diver, you will never forget your first big shark. I felt eternally grateful to encounter this beautiful oceanic whitetip shark in the Red Sea. As we neared the very last minutes of our dive, she and her “entourage” slowly circled our group, peacefully looking at every single one of us, before they took off into the blue again.  Camera Gear: Olympus OM-D E-M5; Olympus M.Zuiko 9-18mm f/4-5.6 lens; Olympus PT-EP13 housing  Settings: f/5.6; 1/250; ISO 200
  Behind the Shot: In early spring, while the mountain peaks around the village of Tragoess are still covered in snow, the meadow lining Grüner See (Green Lake) blooms. Only in later months, when night temperatures don’t fall below freezing, does the snow melt and travel down the creeks to fill up the lake with crystal-clear water. The meadow, with freshly bloomed flowers and rich green grass, gets flooded and for the next two months creates an amazing underwater park.  Camera Gear: Nikon D7000 with Subal housing; Tokina 10-17mm lens  Settings: f/8; 1/60; ISO 400
    Behind the Shot: This image was taken in Greece, on Amorgos Island. There are many caves like this along the coast. The darkness contrasts with the typical blue of the Aegean Sea. Greece is also a paradise for freediving. The settings offer total freedom to the imagination and to the creation of poetic images.
 Camera Gear: Canon 5D Mark II in a Subal CD5 MII housing; Sigma EX 15mm fisheye f/2.8 lens  Settings: f/9; 1/200; ISO 400
  Behind the Shot: In this Cuban archipelago, among the mangroves, lives a small colony of American crocodiles. To make a series of photos I had to go in the water with them for three days, studying the currents, the light and the clarity of the water. Then I waited until the crocodile was in the right position against the backlight. The idea was to have the best contrast between the reflection of the sun and the color of the water.  Camera Gear: Nikon D800E in a Seacam housing; Sigma 15mm fisheye lens; Subtronic Pro 160 strobe  Settings: f/22; 1/160; ISO 125
  Behind the Shot: During a dive in Sharm el Sheikh, I saw an old chain and my attention was caught by a whitebelly damselfish circling around it. When I was close enough, I saw the eggs on it, so I understood that he was oxygenating and protecting them, ensuring reproductive success, and I took some photos of this loving parental male care behavior.
 Gear: Canon EOS 70D in an Easydive Leo3 housing; EF 100mm lens; two Sea&Sea YS-D1 strobes  Settings: f/7.1; 1/200; ISO 100
  Behind the Shot: A humpback whale calf sails through the emerald-blue waters of Tonga, closely accompanied by its colossal mother and escort. As the leviathans circle directly beneath me, the calf ascends toward the surface for fresh air. I tuck back my freediving fins and snap the shot. A split second later, momma surfaces to usher her baby away from the bubble-blowing stranger.
 Camera Gear: Panasonic Lumix GH4; Olympus M.Zuiko ED 8mm F1.8 Fisheye lens; 10bar underwater housing  Settings: f/2.2; 1/500; ISO 200
  Behind the Shot: I captured this flying fish image, “Squadron,” on May 31, 2020, diving around a patch of sargassum just a few feet below the surface while on a black-water dive with Walker’s Dive Charters in Palm Beach, Florida. As I was entering the water, my good friend Lazaro Ruda informed me that there was a school of flying fish on the surface. Within a few minutes I was able to capture the photo.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D850; Nikon 60mm lens; two Inon Z-330 strobes  Settings: f/22; 1/250; ISO 25
   Behind the Shot: Sepiola birostrata is one of two species of cuttlefish resident in the northern Sea of Japan. During the day, it hides on the sandy bottom and can be found only by chance. But at night it goes hunting. Despite their small size [around an inch], these cuttlefish are predators, catching shrimps and crabs. There are usually a lot of them in July, above the sandy bottom, at depths from 15 to 40 feet. I managed to meet this beauty during a night dive. Its color was simply gorgeous.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D500; Nikkor 40mm macro lens; Sea&Sea housing; two Inon Z-220s  Settings: f/18; 1/50; ISO 125
  Behind the Shot: After what seemed like an eternity, the flamingos resumed feeding in the sandy mud where I had been lying motionless since sunrise. As they waded past, they kicked up quite a bit of silt, which landed on my camera’s dome port. This one, being either very inventive or very brave, saw that silt had fallen into my port’s flare petal and began slurping it up, giving me a very brief opportunity to capture those unique filter-feeding serrations inits beak, and that beautiful beady yellow eye.
 Camera Gear: Nikon D850 in a Nauticam housing; Sigma 15mm lens  Settings: f/11; 1/400; ISO 160

note:
65年来，兰德公司的参考书“具有10万个正常偏差的一百万个随机数字”一直享有随机数首选来源的声誉。
布里格斯是一名兰德软件工程师，他在春季翻阅了百万位数字，发现虽然里面的数字确实是相当随机的，但这本备受推崇的书并不完全正确。
布里格斯在加州圣莫尼卡的这家研究机构工作了11年，他说，“我们都对这件具有开创性的65年历史的作品表示敬意和敬意，所以，我要找出我们65年来一直忽视的错误，这个想法让人心烦意乱。”
他说，在现代计算机出现之前，“要获得高质量的随机数真的很难。”这本书改变了一代民调专家、彩票管理员、市场分析师和其他需要随机抽样的人的情况。
note_en:
For 65 years, Rand Corp.’s reference book  “A Million Random Digits with 100,000 Normal Deviates” has enjoyed a reputation as the go-to source for random numbers.
    Mr. Briggs, a Rand software engineer, spent his spring rifling through the million digits and discovered that while the numbers inside are indeed quite random, the venerated book is not quite right.
  “It’s this seminal 65-year-old piece that we all herald and revere,” says Mr. Briggs, an 11-year veteran of the Santa Monica, Calif., research organization, “so the idea that I’m finding errors that we’ve ignored for 65 years is upsetting.”
  Before modern computers, he says, “it was really hard to get high-quality random numbers.” The book changed that for a generation of pollsters, lottery administrators, market analysts and others who needed means of drawing random samples.
title: “一百万个随机数字”是一个数字--克朗彻的“圣经”。现在有一个人暴露了缺陷
title_en: A Million Random Digits Was a Number-Cruncher’s Bible. Now One Has Exposed Flaws

note:
在其175年的历史中，“科学美国人”从未支持过一位总统候选人。今年我们不得不这样做。我们做这件事不是轻率的。
证据和科学表明，唐纳德·特朗普严重损害了美国和美国人民，因为他拒绝接受证据和科学。最具破坏性的例子是他对新冠肺炎大流行的不诚实和无能的反应，截至9月中旬，这场大流行夺去了190,000多名美国人的生命。他还抨击了环境保护、医疗保健，以及帮助这个国家为最大挑战做好准备的研究人员和公共科学机构。这就是为什么我们敦促你们投票给乔·拜登，他提出了基于事实的计划，以保护我们的健康、我们的经济和环境。他提出的这些和其他建议可以让国家回到更安全、更繁荣和更公平的未来的轨道上来。
这场流行病会给任何国家和系统带来压力，但特朗普拒绝提供证据和公共卫生措施在美国是灾难性的。他在1月和2月多次收到关于这种迅速蔓延的疾病的警告，但他没有制定提供防护设备、冠状病毒检测或明确的健康指南的国家战略。对人们进行病毒检测，并追踪他们可能感染的人，是欧洲和亚洲国家如何控制疫情，拯救生命，并成功重新开放企业和学校的。但在美国，特朗普错误地声称，“任何想要测试的人都可以接受测试。”这在3月份是不正确的，整个夏天都是不正确的。特朗普反对增加检测和追踪的250亿美元，这一点直到7月份才出现在大流行缓解法案中。这些失误加速了疾病在全国的传播-特别是在包括有色人种在内的高度脆弱的社区，那里的死亡人数与其他人口相比攀升得不成比例。
这不仅仅是一个测试问题：根据华盛顿大学医学院的预测，如果几乎每个美国人都在公共场合戴口罩，到12月初，它可以拯救大约6.6万人的生命。这样的策略不会伤害任何人。它不会关闭任何业务。这几乎是零成本。但特朗普和他的副总统藐视当地的口罩规定，强调自己在公开露面时不戴口罩。特朗普公开支持那些无视密歇根州、加利福尼亚州和其他地方州长的人，因为他们试图强加社会距离，限制公共活动，以控制病毒。他鼓励佛罗里达州、亚利桑那州和得克萨斯州的州长抵制这些公共卫生措施，并在4月份-再次错误地-表示“大流行最糟糕的日子已经过去”，并无视传染病专家的警告，后者在当时警告称，如果放松安全措施，可能会出现危险的反弹。
当然，反弹来了，全国的病例在6月份上升了46%，死亡人数增加了21%。在特朗普的误导之后，各州公布了新的单日新高，阳性检测的百分比也高于那些没有误导的州。到7月初，德克萨斯州的几家医院已经挤满了新冠肺炎的病人。各州不得不再次关闭，付出了巨大的经济代价。在巨大的失业浪潮之后-超过3000万人和无数倒闭的企业-已经摧毁了这个国家，大约31%的工人第二次被解雇。在每个阶段，特朗普都拒绝接受一个明确的教训，即控制疾病，而不是淡化它，是经济重新开放和复苏的道路。
根据特朗普对记者鲍勃·伍德沃德的录音声明，特朗普在这种疾病的致命威胁上一再向公众撒谎，称这并不是一个严重的担忧，当他知道这种疾病更具杀伤力和高度传播性时，他说这并不是一个严重的担忧，而且在他知道它更具致命性和高度传播性的时候，他说这就像流感​。他的谎言鼓励人们从事危险的行为，进一步传播病毒，并在认真对待这一威胁的美国人和那些相信特朗普谎言的人之间制造了裂痕。白宫甚至制作了一份备忘录，抨击美国首屈一指的传染病内科医生安东尼·福奇(Anthony Fuci)的专业知识，这是一种卑鄙的企图，目的是进一步播下不信任的种子。
对于美国百年来最严重的公共卫生危机，特朗普的反应是“我根本不承担任何责任。”相反，他指责其他国家和他的白宫前任，后者在疫情开始前三年离任。
但特朗普拒绝查看证据并采取相应行动的行为超出了病毒的范畴。他多次试图废除平价医疗法案，但没有提供其他选择；全面的医疗保险对于减少疾病至关重要。特朗普提议削减美国国立卫生研究院(National Institutes Of Health)、国家科学基金会(National Science Foundation)和疾病控制和预防中心(Centers For Disease Control And Preven)10亿美元
特朗普还不断推动取消美国环境保护局(Environmental Protection Agency)的健康规定，使人们面临更大的污染导致心肺疾病的风险。他已经用行业代表取代了机构顾问委员会中的科学家。在持续否认现实的过程中，特朗普阻碍了美国应对气候变化的准备工作，错误地声称气候变化不存在，并退出了缓解气候变化的国际协议。气候变化已经导致与高温相关的死亡人数上升，严重风暴、野火和极端洪水增加。
相比之下，乔·拜登准备好了控制新冠肺炎、改善医疗保健、减少碳排放以及恢复合法科学在政策制定中的作用的计划。他寻求专业知识，并将这些知识转化为坚实的政策建议。
在谈到新冠肺炎时，他正确地说：“在我们的公共健康和我们的经济之间‘选择’是错误的。如果我们不战胜病毒，我们将永远无法恢复全面的经济实力。“。拜登计划扩大一个国家考试委员会，这个机构将有权命令公共和私人资源提供更多的考试，并将它们带到所有社区。他还希望建立一个10万人的公共卫生工作队，其中许多人在大流行危机期间被解雇，作为接触追踪者和其他卫生工作。他将指示职业健康和安全管理局执行工作场所安全标准，以避免发生在肉类加工厂和疗养院的那种致命疫情。虽然特朗普威胁要不顾病毒的危险，扣留没有重新开学的学区的资金，但拜登希望花费340亿美元，帮助学校进行安全的面对面教学和远程学习。
拜登正在听取一个团体对这些公共卫生问题的建议，其中包括流行病学家、儿科医生、前美国食品和药物管理局(FDA)局长大卫·凯斯勒(David Kessler)，乔治敦大学(Georgetown University)免疫学家兼全球卫生安全专家丽贝卡·卡茨(Rebecca Katz)，以及宾夕法尼亚大学(University Of Pennsylvania)生物伦理学家伊泽基尔·伊曼纽尔(Ezekiel Emanuel)。这不包括相信外星人和揭穿病毒疗法的医生，特朗普称其中一种疗法“非常受人尊敬”和“壮观”。
拜登有一个家庭和护理倡议，认识到这是持续的公共卫生和经济复苏的关键。他的计划包括增加儿童保育人员的工资，并为儿童建设新的设施，因为负担不起高质量的护理使工作人员远离经济，并给家庭带来巨大压力。
在环境和气候变化方面，拜登希望到2035年在零排放电力部门投入2万亿美元，建造节能结构和车辆，推动太阳能和风能发电，建立研究机构来开发安全的核能和碳捕获技术，等等。他的竞选团队声称，这项投资将为美国工人创造200万个就业机会，气候计划将通过取消特朗普的企业减税来部分支付。美国历史上处于不利地位的社区将获得这些能源和基础设施好处的40%。
目前还不确定拜登将能够实现其中的多少以及他的其他雄心壮志；这在很大程度上取决于国会将要起草和通过的法律。但他敏锐地意识到，我们必须注意大量的研究，这些研究表明了如何从当前的危机中恢复过来，并成功地应对未来的挑战。
尽管特朗普和他的盟友试图制造障碍，阻止人们在11月通过邮寄或亲自投票的方式安全投票，但我们克服这些障碍并投票是至关重要的。现在是时候把特朗普赶下台，选举拜登了，他有跟踪数据和以科学为指导的记录。
编者按(9/15/20)：本文在2020年10月号的“科学美国人”上发表后进行了编辑，以反映最近的报道。
note_en:
Scientific American has never endorsed a presidential candidate in its 175-year history. This year we are compelled to do so. We do not do this lightly.
  The evidence and the science show that Donald Trump has badly damaged the U.S. and its people—because he rejects evidence and science. The most devastating example is his dishonest and inept response to the COVID-19 pandemic, which  cost more than 190,000 Americans their lives by the middle of September. He has also attacked environmental protections, medical care, and the researchers and public science agencies that help this country prepare for its greatest challenges. That is why we urge you to vote for Joe Biden, who is offering fact-based plans to protect our health, our economy and the environment. These and other proposals he has put forth can set the country back on course for a safer, more prosperous and more equitable future.
  The pandemic would strain any nation and system, but Trump's rejection of evidence and public health measures have been catastrophic in the U.S. He was  warned many times in January and February about the onrushing disease, yet he did not develop a national strategy to provide protective equipment, coronavirus testing or clear health guidelines.  Testing people for the virus, and tracing those they may have infected, is how countries in Europe and Asia have gained control over their outbreaks, saved lives, and successfully reopened businesses and schools. But in the U.S., Trump claimed, falsely, that “ anybody that wants a test can get a test.” That was  untrue in March and remained untrue through the summer. Trump opposed  $25 billion for increased testing and tracing that was in a pandemic relief bill as late as July. These lapses accelerated the spread of disease through the country—particularly in highly vulnerable communities that include people of color, where  deaths climbed disproportionately to those in the rest of the population.
  It wasn't just a testing problem: if almost everyone in the U.S. wore masks in public, it  could save about 66,000 lives by the beginning of December, according to projections from the University of Washington School of Medicine. Such a strategy would hurt no one. It would close no business. It would cost next to nothing. But Trump and his vice president flouted local mask rules, making it a point not to wear masks themselves in public appearances. Trump has openly supported people who ignored governors in  Michigan and  California and elsewhere as they tried to impose social distancing and restrict public activities to control the virus. He encouraged governors in  Florida, Arizona and Texas who resisted these public health measures, saying in April—again, falsely—that “ the worst days of the pandemic are behind us” and ignoring infectious disease experts who warned at the time of a dangerous rebound if safety measures were loosened.
  And of course, the rebound came, with  cases across the nation rising by 46 percent and deaths increasing by 21 percent in June. The states that followed Trump's misguidance posted new daily highs and higher percentages of positive tests than those that did not. By early July  several hospitals in Texas were full of COVID-19 patients. States had to close up again, at tremendous economic cost. About  31 percent of workers were laid off a second time, following the giant wave of unemployment—more than 30 million people and countless shuttered businesses—that had already decimated the country. At every stage, Trump has rejected the unmistakable lesson that controlling the disease, not downplaying it, is the path to economic reopening and recovery.
  Trump repeatedly lied to the public about the deadly threat of the disease, saying it was not a serious concern and “ this is like a flu​” when he knew it was more lethal and highly transmissible, according to his taped statements to journalist Bob Woodward. His lies encouraged people to engage in risky behavior, spreading the virus further, and have  driven wedges between Americans who take the threat seriously and those who believe Trump's falsehoods. The White House even produced a  memo attacking the expertise of the nation's leading infectious disease physician, Anthony Fauci, in a despicable attempt to sow further distrust.
  Trump's reaction to America's worst public health crisis in a century has been to say “ I don't take responsibility at all.” Instead he blamed other countries and his White House predecessor, who left office three years before the pandemic began.
  But Trump's refusal to look at the evidence and act accordingly extends beyond the virus. He has repeatedly tried to get rid of the Affordable Care Act while offering no alternative; comprehensive  medical insurance is essential to reduce illness. Trump has proposed billion-dollar cuts to the  National Institutes of Health, the National Science Foundation, and the  Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, agencies that increase our scientific knowledge and strengthen us for future challenges. Congress has countermanded his reductions. Yet he keeps trying,  slashing programs that would ready us for future pandemics and withdrawing from the World Health Organization. These and other actions increase the risk that new diseases will surprise and devastate us again.
  Trump also keeps pushing to  eliminate health rules from the Environmental Protection Agency, putting people at more risk for heart and lung disease caused by pollution. He has replaced scientists on agency advisory boards with industry representatives. In his ongoing denial of reality, Trump has hobbled U.S. preparations for climate change,  falsely claiming that it does not exist and pulling out of international agreements to mitigate it. The changing climate is already causing a rise in  heat-related deaths and an increase in severe storms, wildfires and extreme flooding.
  Joe Biden, in contrast, comes prepared with plans to control COVID-19, improve health care, reduce carbon emissions and restore the role of legitimate science in policy making. He solicits expertise and has turned that knowledge into solid policy proposals.
  On COVID-19, he  states correctly that “it is wrong to talk about ‘choosing' between our public health and our economy.... If we don't beat the virus, we will never get back to full economic strength.” Biden plans to ramp up a national testing board, a body that would have the authority to command both public and private resources to supply more tests and get them to all communities. He also wants to establish a Public Health Job Corps of 100,000 people, many of whom have been laid off during the pandemic crisis, to serve as contact tracers and in other health jobs. He will direct the Occupational Health and Safety Administration to enforce workplace safety standards to avoid the kind of deadly outbreaks that have occurred at meat-processing plants and nursing homes. While Trump threatened to withhold money from school districts that did not reopen, regardless of the danger from the virus,  Biden wants to spend $34 billion to help schools conduct safe in-person instruction as well as remote learning.
  Biden is getting advice on these public health issues from a group that includes David Kessler, epidemiologist, pediatrician and former U.S. Food and Drug Administration chief; Rebecca Katz, immunologist and global health security specialist at Georgetown University; and Ezekiel Emanuel, bioethicist at the University of Pennsylvania. It does not include physicians who believe in aliens and debunked virus therapies, one of whom  Trump has called “very respected” and “spectacular.”
  Biden has  a family and caregiving initiative, recognizing this as key to a sustained public health and economic recovery. His plans include increased salaries for child care workers and construction of new facilities for children because the inability to afford quality care keeps workers out of the economy and  places enormous strains on families.
  On  the environment and climate change, Biden wants to spend $2 trillion on an emissions-free power sector by 2035, build energy-efficient structures and vehicles, push solar and wind power, establish research agencies to develop safe nuclear power and carbon capture technologies, and more. The investment will produce two million jobs for U.S. workers,  his campaign claims, and the climate plan will be partly paid by eliminating Trump's corporate tax cuts. Historically disadvantaged communities in the U.S. will receive 40 percent of these energy and infrastructure benefits.
  It is not certain how many of these and his other ambitions Biden will be able to accomplish; much depends on laws to be written and passed by Congress. But he is acutely aware that we must heed the abundant research showing ways to recover from our present crises and successfully cope with future challenges.
  Although  Trump and his allies have tried to create obstacles that prevent people from casting ballots safely in November, either by mail or in person, it is crucial that we surmount them and vote. It's time to move Trump out and elect Biden, who has a record of following the data and being guided by science.
  Editor’s Note (9/15/20): This article has been edited after its publication in the October 2020 issue of Scientific American  to reflect recent reporting.
title: 科学美国人支持乔·拜登

note:
谁讲的故事最有说服力，谁就赢了。这不是个好主意。就是那个能引起人们注意并让他们点头的故事。
有些东西可能是真实的，但在上下文中可能是无稽之谈。糟糕的想法通常至少有一些真理的种子，给他们的追随者以信心。
告诉人们他们想听的话，你可能会无限期地错，而不会受到惩罚。
伍德罗·威尔逊(Woodrow Wilson)说，政府“对达尔文负责，而不是对牛顿负责。”这是个有用的主意。每件事都要对这两者中的一个负责，你必须知道某些东西是会随着时间的推移而适应和变化，还是永远保持不变。
行为很难改正。当人们说他们已经吸取了教训时，他们低估了他们之前的错误有多少是由面对同样情况时会卷土重来的情绪造成的。
历史学家威尔·杜兰特(Will Durant)说：“逻辑是人类的发明，可能会被宇宙忽视。”这就是为什么预测很难。
擅长某件事并不能保证会有回报。它甚至没有承诺要恭维你。这个世界得到的回报是稀缺，所以重要的是你能做别人不擅长的事情。
这个世界是由概率支配的，但人们的思维是黑白分明的，是对还是错-它发生了还是没有发生？-因为它更容易。
亨利·卢斯说：“给我看一个自认为客观的人，我就让你看到一个自欺欺人的人。”人们看到他们想看到的，听到他们想听到的，通过他们自己独特的生活经历的镜头来看待世界。
当人们感到惊讶时，他们就会学到东西。不是当他们读到正确的答案，或者被告知做错了，而是当他们的下巴掉到地板上的时候。
大多数领域只有几条法律。很多理论、预感、观察、想法、趋势和规则。但是法律--总是正确的，总是正确的--是很少见的。
唯一比认为所有与你意见不同的人都错了更糟糕的事情是相反的：被那些需要或想要你不想要的东西的人的建议说服。
简单的解释即使是错的也很有吸引力。“这很复杂”即使是正确的，也不具有说服力。
利己主义是世界上最强大的力量。这可能是好的，因为每个人的利益一致的情况是不可阻挡的；坏的是因为人们愿意以牺牲他人为代价谋取自己的利益是如此诱人。
历史源远流长。几乎所有的事情以前都做过了。角色和场景发生了变化，但行为和结果几乎没有变化。“当你没有接触到历史时，一切都感觉前所未有。”
不要指望从非常有才华的人那里获得平衡。在一件事上特别擅长的人往往在另一件事上也非常糟糕，这是因为过度自信和特殊技能占用了精神带宽。技能也有两个方面：当以你喜欢的独特方式思考世界的人也以你不喜欢的独特方式思考世界时，任何人都不应该感到震惊。
进步太慢而不能注意到，挫折发生得太快而不能忽视。一夜之间有很多悲剧，但不会有一夜之间的奇迹。增长是由复利推动的，这总是需要时间的。破坏是由单点故障(可能在几秒钟内发生)和信心丧失(可能在瞬间发生)驱动的。
发现别人的错误要比发现自己的错误容易得多。我们仅仅根据别人的行为来判断他们，但当我们判断自己时，我们会进行内部对话，为我们的错误和糟糕的决定辩护。
声誉是双向的，因为人们希望与成功者交往，避免失败者。
历史是由令人惊讶的事件推动的，预测是由可预测的事件推动的。这不是一个容易解决的问题。
note_en:
The person who tells the most compelling story wins. Not the best idea. Just the story that catches people’s attention and gets them to nod their heads.
 Something can be factually true but contextually nonsense. Bad ideas often have at least some seed of truth that gives their followers confidence.
 Tell people what they want to hear and you can be wrong indefinitely without penalty.
 Woodrow Wilson said government “is accountable to Darwin, not to Newton.” It’s a useful idea. Everything is accountable to one of the two, and you have to know whether something adapts and changes over time or perpetually stays the same.
 Behavior is hard to fix. When people say they’ve learned their lesson they underestimate how much of their previous mistake was caused by emotions that will return when faced with the same circumstances.
 “Logic is an invention of man and may be ignored by the universe,” historian Will Durant says. That’s why forecasting is hard.
 Being good at something doesn’t promise rewards. It doesn’t even promise a compliment. What’s rewarded in the world is scarcity, so what matters is what you can do that other people are bad at.
 The world is governed by probability, but people think in black and white, right or wrong – did it happen or did it not? – because it’s easier.
 Henry Luce said, “Show me a man who thinks he’s objective and I’ll show you a man who’s deceiving himself.” People see what they want to see, hear what they want to hear, and view the world through the lens of their own unique life experiences.
 People learn when they’re surprised. Not when they read the right answer, or are told they’re doing it wrong, but when their jaw hits the floor.
 Most fields have only a few laws. Lots of theories, hunches, observations, ideas, trends, and rules. But laws – things that are always true, all the time – are rare.
 The only thing worse than thinking everyone who disagrees with you is wrong is the opposite: being persuaded by the advice of those who need or want something you don’t.
 Simple explanations are appealing even when they’re wrong. “It’s complicated” isn’t persuasive even when it’s right.
 Self-interest is the most powerful force in the world. Which can be great, because situations where everyone’s interests align are unstoppable; bad because people’s willingness to benefit themselves at the expense of others is so seductive.
 History is deep. Almost everything has been done before. The characters and scenes change, but the behaviors and outcomes rarely do. “ Everything feels unprecedented when you haven’t engaged with history.”
 Don’t expect balance from very talented people. People who are exceptionally good at one thing tend to be exceptionally bad at another, due to overconfidence and mental bandwidth taken up by the exceptional skill. Skills also have two sides: No one should be shocked when people who think about the world in unique ways you like also think about the world in unique ways you don’t like.
 Progress happens too slowly to notice, setbacks happen too fast to ignore. There are lots of overnight tragedies, but no overnight miracles. Growth is driven by compounding, which always takes time. Destruction is driven by single points of failure, which can happen in seconds, and loss of confidence, which can happen in an instant.
 It is way easier to spot other people’s mistakes than your own. We judge others based solely on their actions, but when judging ourselves we have an internal dialogue that justifies our mistakes and bad decisions.
 Reputations have momentum in both directions, because people want to associate with winners and avoid losers.
 History is driven by surprising events, forecasting is driven by predictable ones. It’s not an easy problem to solve.

2020年5月7日，政府采取行动，有偏见地驳回了对弗林将军的起诉。ECF编号。198.。在此案在有偏见的情况下被驳回之前，政府有持续的义务向辩方提供所有证据，证明弗林将军无罪，证明政府在许多方面的不当行为促成了这起错误的起诉，或以其他方式有利于辩方。
，373美国。83(1963)。辩方有持续的义务记录这一驳回-特别是考虑到本法院前所未有的程序和立场。昨晚深夜，政府又出示了一批文件-这是自2020年4月以来的第五份无罪证据，也是自提交大规模驳回动议以来的第三份。ECF第198、231、237号。根据政府的生产函件：“这些文件包括。
前副助理局长彼得·斯特佐克(23501&23503)和前副局长安德鲁·麦凯布(23502)的手写便条；以及参与弗林事件的联邦调查局分析师之间的内部短信(23504-23516)；。。前父亲斯特佐克和丽莎·佩奇(23516-23540)之间的其他短信。“。这41页的补充证据证明(I)他是无辜的；(Ii)没有任何犯罪行为；(Iii)政府在调查弗林将军时的不当行为；以及(Iv)继续检方在压制有利于辩方的证据方面的不当行为，违反了。
秩序。ECF编号。20.这些文件提供了司法部和联邦调查局最高层的特工和其他人早就知道的信息；特别顾问和联邦调查局长期隐瞒的信息。这一证据显示了FBI和司法部肆无忌惮的蓄意不当行为-与民族英雄的生命玩游戏。它否定了起诉虚假陈述罪所需的多个基本要素，以及任何被认为与弗林·英特尔集团(Flynn Intel Group)审查有关费特胡拉·居伦(Fethullah Gulen)和穆斯林兄弟会(Muslim Brotherhood)的开源信息有关的“罪行”。这一引人注目的新成果表明，2016年8月，联邦调查局(FBI)的分析人士讨论了一些特工对克林顿当选总统的偏好--这是一个已知的数字--“而不是像[T]Rump这样的外卡。”
在2016年10月FBI的“Lync”信息系统上交换的信息中，FBI员工就“Crossfire路演”交换了信息，称他们“有兴趣看看这一切会如何发展”。他们确切地知道情况有多糟糕：“我告诉你，如果这件事被”信息自由法“批准，将会有一些尖锐的问题被问到。”值得注意的是，对弗林将军的“调查”于2016年11月8日首次结案。联邦调查局特工写道：“他说关闭Razor”，“很高兴他们要关闭Razor。”然而[编辑]“却保持沉默，所以谁知道他会想要什么。”那是安迪[麦凯布]吗？
弗林将军有第六修正案对针对他的诉讼公开赦免的权利，美国人民有权知道邪恶行为的真相和犯下这种行为的人。辩方反对任何编辑，并要求政府将这些文件未经编辑地归档到公共记录中。

UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT  DISTRICT OF COLUMBIA  UNITED STATES OF AMERICA  v.  MICHAEL T. FLYNN,  Defendant.  Criminal Action No. 17-232-EGS  THIRD SUPPLEMENT IN SUPPORT OF AGREED DISMISSAL
 On May 7, 2020, the Government moved to dismiss with prejudice the  prosecution of  General Flynn.  ECF No.  198.  Until this  case is  dismissed w ith  prejudice, the Government has a continuing obligation to provide to the defense all  evidence that is exculpatory of General Flynn, establishes misconduct by the  Government in its many capacities that contributed to this wrongful prosecution, or  otherwise is favorable to the defense.
  ,  373 U.S .  83  (1963).  The  defense has a continuing obligation to make a record that mandates this dismissal—  especially in view of this court’s unprecedented procedures and position.  Late last night, the Government produced yet another tranche of  documents—its fifth production of exculpatory evidence since April of 2020, and its  third since  filing its  massive Motion  to Dismiss.  ECF Nos.  198, 231,  237.  According  to  the  Government’s  production  correspondence:  “The  documents  include
   handwritten notes of former Deputy Assistant Director Peter Strzok (23501 & 23503)  and former Deputy Director Andrew McCabe (23502); and internal text messages  between FBI analysts who worked on the Flynn matter (23504-  23516); . . . additional  text messages between former  DAD Strzok and  Lisa Page (23516-23540).”  The 41  pages of additional evidence demonstrate (i) his innocence; (ii) the absence of any  crime; (iii) government misconduct in the investigation of General Flynn; and (iv)  continuing  prosecutorial misconduct in the suppression of evidence favorable to the   defense in violation of
    order.  ECF  No.  20.  These documents provide information long known to the agents and others at  the highest levels of the Department of Justice and the FBI; information long  concealed by the  Special Counsel  and FBI.  This evidence shows  outrageous,  deliberate misconduct by FBI and DOJ—playing games with the life of a national  hero.  It  negates  multiple essential elements required for the prosecution of a false  statement offense and any “offense” even considered in relationship to Flynn Intel  Group’s review of open source information regarding Fethullah Gulen and the  Muslim Brotherhood.  This remarkable new production shows that in August of 2016, the FBI  analysts discussed the preference of some agents for a Clinton Presidency—a known  quantity—“instead of a wild card like [T]rump.”
   In messages exchanged on the FBI’s “Lync” messaging system in October of  2016, FBI employees exchanged messages about the “Crossfire Road Show,” stating  that they were “interested to see how this  all plays out.”  They knew exactly how bad  it was: “I’m tellying man, if this thing ever gets FOIA’d, there are going to be some  tough questions asked.”  Notably, the “investigation” of General Flynn was first closed by November 8,  2016.  The FBI agents w rite: “he said  shut down Razor”  and “so glad  they’re closing  Razor.”  However [redacted] “was silent  though, so who  knows what he  will want.”  Was that Andy [McCabe]?
    General Flynn  has a  Sixth Amendment  right to  a public  exoneration regarding  the proceedings against him, and the American people have a right to know the trut h  about the nefarious conduct and the people  who committed it.  The defense objects to  any redactions and demands that the Government file these documents unredacted  on the public record.

note:
几周前，当佛罗里达大学大二学生夏延·基廷(Cheyenne Keting)在家中统计考试时感到一阵恶心时，她看着她的网络摄像头，问另一边的陌生人：可以吐在我的桌子上吗？
他答应了。因此，在长达两个小时的测试进行到一半时，她吐到了柳条篮子里，用毯子轻轻擦了一下乱七八糟的东西，然后马上回到了工作岗位。在测试期间，她的每一个动作都受到了作弊的仔细检查，不允许上厕所。陌生人看到了一切。测试结束后，他说她可以自由注销了。只有这样她才能把自己弄干净。
像这样的“在线监考”服务已经监管了数百万的美国大学考试，当学生在家考试时，他们会监听学生的相机、麦克风和电脑屏幕。现在，随着冠状病毒大流行关闭了数以千计的美国学校，这些公司正在享受着一波新业务的热潮，高管们正在竞相在一些人所说的千载难逢的机会中抓住新客户。
这些公司聘请的现场监考人员确保考生遵守一套严格的规则。他们观察学生的脸，倾听他们的谈话，并可以要求他们将相机对准房间周围，以证明他们的诚实。一些公司还使用面部识别、眼球跟踪和其他软件，声称可以检测作弊行为，并对学生的“学术诚信”进行评级。
例如，在屏幕外看太久可能会提高考生的“怀疑”分数，有可能导致他们考试不及格。这些公司与学校签订了合同，其中包括部分费用，但许多公司也向学生收取费用：一家名为ProctorU的公司向学生收取每次考试约15美元的费用，而另一家公司Proctorio提供100美元的“终身费”。
“这简直是疯了。我不应该高兴的。我知道很多人现在过得不是很好，但对我们来说--我甚至无法解释。“普罗科托里奥的首席执行官Mike Olsen在接受采访时说。“仅今年一年，我们的价值就可能增加4到5倍。”
这种爆炸性的增长揭示了，随着隐私问题与现代大流行前所未有的现实发生冲突，这可能是美国大规模监控的关键时刻。数十万名学生从大学被遣送回家，数百万名高中生看到他们当地的学校在今年剩下的时间里关闭。
随着越来越多的学校推动追踪学生在校园内的位置以及他们在家中的测试行为，教育倡导者担心这些系统正在侵犯学生的个人生活，并将学习的做法减少为法医调查，在被证明是正确的之前，学生被推定为作弊者。
“要参加考试，你需要让陌生人有你房间的录像带？”你在跟我开玩笑吗？“。专门研究教育技术的非营利组织消费者报告(Consumer Reports)的研究员比尔·菲茨杰拉德(Bill Fitzgerald)说。
“这些平台之所以存在，是因为它们在兜售一种不能信任学生的说法，”他说。“在这里损失最大的是学生，他们离决定最远。…。学生们花了数万美元让他们的高等学府把他们的票卖光了。“。
之前，被系统侵扰的眼睛困扰的学生可以选择以老式的方式考试，在教室或考试中心。但随着校园关闭，学生的参与实际上已经成为强制性的-就在他们期末考试之前。
这些系统已经让像尼尔·比特纳(Neil Buettner)这样的学生感到不安。比特纳现年28岁，是马里兰州丘奇维尔哈福德社区学院(Harford Community College)的一名海军陆战队退伍军人，他在参加微观经济学考试之前，被在线Proctor服务Honorlock提出的要求激怒了。
“它在谈论它想要如何访问我的电脑、我的麦克风和网络摄像头。监视我周围的房间，扫描我的房间。它要扫描我的身份证！“。他在接受采访时说。当他的教授说他没有亲自参加考试的选择时，他选择了退课。“这只是一个巨大的倒退，”Buettner说。“每个人都为了病毒放弃了自己的自由。”
这些担忧并没有削弱像Proctorio这样的公司的吸引力。Proctorio在美国和欧洲的四个销售办事处设有员工，在去年12月的高峰期管理着120多万名学生。奥尔森说，他预计到本学年结束时，他们的业务可能会增加两倍以上。
该公司通常在一年内增加100所新大学作为客户，现在每天大约有120条线索。通常会经历长达一个月的谈判的大型大学现在希望在几天内匆忙通过交易。他说，不情愿的管理人员和教授们突然发现“他们正被迫”尝试。
冠状病毒封锁还迫使一些公司允许他们的主管远程工作，而不是在有监督的办公室里工作，这让隐私倡导者对谁可以进入学生卧室的视频流发出了警告。一家名为Examity的公司最近关闭了其在印度的Proctor中心，该公司发布了全职承包商的工作清单，这些承包商最早将于本月开始观察考生。
该软件对学生的侵略性要求也在一些教授中引发了愤怒。加州大学圣巴巴拉分校(University Of California At Santa Barbara)的一个教职员工团体上个月给校园领导写了一封信，认为采用ProctorU可能会把大学变成“一个监控工具”。
“我们认识…。我们必须接受网上移民的权衡和不幸的方面，“他们写道。“这不是其中之一。我们不愿意为了一次带回家的期末考试的权宜之计而牺牲我们学生的隐私和数字权利。“。(ProctorU的一名律师回复了一封信，威胁要对该组织的“诽谤性通信”采取法律行动。)。
ProctorU的首席执行官斯科特·麦克法兰(Scott McFarland)说，怀疑者的人数超过了新出现兴趣的学校领导人：上个月的一天，他的办公室接到了近1000个来自教育工作者询问这项服务的电话。他说，该公司在很大程度上与大学和私立高中合作，但大流行开启了扩展到小学考试的可能性。
“这是一个缓慢的浪潮，但这改变了一切，让它更像是海啸事件，”他补充说。“在我们以前没有真正追逐过的地方，机会实在太多了。”
在ProctorU测试开始时，学生们被告知要向Proctor出示他们的学生证，他们的房间和桌子的顶部，以证明他们手头没有任何作弊材料。在考试期间，监考人员通过学生的麦克风进行监听，以确保他或她不会向看不见的人寻求帮助。
监考员可以进入考生的电脑屏幕，如果考生做了一些不可接受的事情，比如复制和粘贴文本，或者打开一个新的浏览器标签，监考人员会收到警报。一个视频系统分析学生的眼睛：如果他们在一分钟内连续四秒钟看向屏幕外超过两次，这一动作将被标记为可疑事件-这是一个暗示，他们可能是在参考屏幕外发布的笔记。
为了确保正确的考生参加考试，该软件使用面部识别软件将他们与身份证上的图像进行匹配。考试过程中会进行随机扫描，以防止另一名考生跳进来。
该公司还通过打字测试来验证身份：学生可能会在学期初被要求键入140个单词，然后在测试之前再次输入，以验证学生击键的速度和节奏。任何不符之处都可以标记出来，以便更仔细地检查。
人类监考员看着考试的每一秒，尽管学生看不到监考员的脸。麦克法兰说，在以前的软件版本中，学生可以看到正在看他们的人，但“总是会出现令人毛骨悚然的因素。”如果监考人员怀疑作弊，他们会提醒一名更具攻击性的专家，即所谓的“干预主义者”，后者可以要求学生将他或她的网络摄像头对准可疑区域，否则将面临学术处罚。
督察通常在阿拉巴马州、加利福尼亚州、印度、牙买加、巴拿马和菲律宾的11个中心之一工作。但该公司表示，随着其中许多办公室的关闭，它将在加拿大开设后备中心，雇佣100多名新员工，并指导许多监工在家工作。
麦克法兰说，ProctorU去年监督了超过75万名学生的200万次测试，收集了多年来关于学生15种“行为作弊类型”的数据。学生的考试被现场直播并录制下来，供以后审查：麦克法兰说，最严重的违规者已经将他们的视频编辑在一起，形成了他所说的作弊“名人堂”。
ProctorU的竞争对手以不同的策略提供类似的反作弊监控。总部位于佛罗里达州的Honorlock公司首席执行官迈克尔·亨利普(Michael Hemplep)表示，该公司“查询激增”，该公司使用软件寻找“企图的不诚实”，然后派一名人类监听器进行进一步审查。
Proctorio更进一步，使用完全软件驱动的方法。在学生同意让Proctorio监控他们的网络摄像头、麦克风、桌面或“维护诚信所需的任何其他手段”后，系统会跟踪他们的语言和眼动、完成测试所需的时间以及点击鼠标的次数。然后，它会给教授一份自动报告，根据“怀疑级别”和测试“异常”的数量对考生进行排名。被计算机认为不值得信任的学生被用红色编码，并被赋予一个由两个影子组成的图标，这让人想起“疯狂”杂志成名的卡通“间谍大战间谍”(Spy vs.Spy)。
使用Proctorio的犹他州立大学(Utah State University)学术测试服务主管克里斯·戴利(Chris Dayley)笑着将该软件描述为“有点像我们刚刚合法化的间谍软件”。尽管许多学生鄙视被监视的感觉，但该公司首席执行官奥尔森表示，如果这有助于保护考试，这种不适是值得的。“我们是警察，”他说。
在一个社会疏远的时代，这些公司都在争先恐后地表明他们有解决大学考试危机的办法。他们的网站包括与冠状病毒相关的广告、介绍性定价优惠和精简的合同，用洪诺洛克的话说，就是“消除法律干预和责任担忧的需要”。
奥本大学(Auburn University)比吉奥加强教学中心(Biggio Center For The Enhanced Of Education And Learning At Auburn University)的执行主任阿西姆·阿里(Asim Ali)表示，随着阿拉巴马州庞大的校区在春季学期剩下的时间里人去楼空，学校正在为本月晚些时候的一场突袭做准备，届时将有20多万场期末考试由霍诺洛克和普罗克托尔大学监督。这些系统将覆盖该大学2.3万多名本科生参加的几乎每一项测试，无论他们之前是否同意这项技术。
“这是一种危机情况，”阿里说。“危急时刻需要采取孤注一掷的措施。”
但一些教授和隐私权倡导者担心，这股狂热正导致大学批准大数据窃取，这可能会让他们的学生暴露在风险之中。
克雷顿大学(Creighton University)副教授盖伊·麦克亨德里(Guy McHendry)说，“学生们被要求同意这些决定，但他们没有不同意的实际权力。”克雷顿大学在一些监考中使用了Examity。“由于我们做这件事的紧迫性，我们真的没有时间去消化这些公司将要做的事情的所有影响。”
这些公司保留了他们从学生电脑和卧室收集的大部分信息的权利。ProctorU针对加州考生的隐私政策显示，该公司与监考人员和学校共享大量敏感的学生数据：他们的家庭地址；他们的工作、父母和公民身份的详细信息；医疗记录，包括他们的体重、健康状况和身体或精神残疾；以及生物特征数据，包括指纹、面部图像、语音记录和“虹膜或视网膜扫描”。
该公司表示，它与一组网站分析提供商共享考生的浏览历史、搜索和在线互动，但没有透露这些提供商的名字。该公司还表示，它保留与学校分享学生的所有视频和音频记录的权利，以确保“不违反考试协议”。该政策规定，学生数据将“保留”至“必要的时间”。(该公司执行长麦克法兰称，不向第三方出售任何信息。)。
教育倡导者表示，他们担心这些公司和学校将如何使用这种越来越私密的学生私生活观点。他们还质疑，这些系统是否会因为一些他们无法控制的事情而不公平地惩罚学生：技术故障、紧张的抽搐或其他走进视线的室友。“所有这些监控，”博客“黑客教育”(Hack Education)的作者奥黛丽·沃特斯(Audrey Watters)说，“对于这种非常人性化的教学实践来说，并不理想。”
在留言板上，学生们声称，在漫长的考试中，他们因伸展身体抓钢笔或让眼睛游移而被标记。一名学生在Reddit上表示，他们的教授指责他们在一次由荣誉锁监督的考试中作弊，因为他们在手工解数学题时一直在屏幕外看。
在TikTok上，学生们发布了一些视频，视频内容包括被监考老师无情地监视，考试期间“精神崩溃”，以及考试计时器倒计时时哭泣。其他人分享了作弊的方法(“帽子”、“哈欠”)，并开玩笑说自己被拍到在镜头前跳挑衅性的舞蹈。
大规模转向实验测试系统让一些管理员担心接下来会发生什么。华盛顿州立大学(Washington State University)负责学术推广的副校长大卫·西莱(David Cillay)表示，他担心该州农村地区的学生，那里的互联网连接参差不齐。华盛顿州立大学的期末考试定于5月初举行。“我们正在进入这个环境，在学期中期，那时我们还没有准备好让学生理解所有的技术，”他说。
但许多大学领导表示，没有时间犹豫不决了。德克萨斯大学奥斯汀分校(University Of Texas At Austin)教授米赫兰·阿罗安(Mihran Aroian)表示，他预计未来几周将有数百名新生通过ProctorU参加他的组织行为基础和管理课程考试，他没有针对对公司要求感到不安的学生的后备计划。
他说，这种病毒可能标志着美国教育的一个“临界点”。“我们不能假设这件事结束后一切都会恢复正常，”他补充说。“这是一个全新的世界。”
note_en:
When University of Florida sophomore Cheyenne Keating felt a rush of nausea a few weeks ago during her at-home statistics exam, she looked into her webcam and asked the stranger on the other side: Is it okay to throw up at my desk?
  He said yes. So halfway through the two-hour test, during which her every movement was scrutinized for cheating and no bathroom breaks were permitted, she vomited into a wicker basket, dabbed the mess with a blanket and got right back to work. The stranger saw everything. When the test was finished, he said she was free to log off. Only then could she clean herself up.
 “Online proctor” services like these have already policed millions of American college exams, tapping into students’ cameras, microphones and computer screens when they take their tests at home. Now these companies are enjoying a rush of new business as the  coronavirus pandemic closes thousands of American schools, and executives are racing to capture new clients during what some are calling a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity.
   The live proctors these companies hire ensure test-takers abide by a strict set of rules. They watch the students’ faces, listen to them talk and can demand they aim their cameras around the room to prove their honesty. Some companies also use facial-recognition, eye-tracking and other software that purports to detect cheating and rates the students’ “academic integrity.”
 Looking off-screen for too long, for instance, can raise a test-taker’s “suspicion” score, potentially leading them to fail the exam. The companies sign contracts with the schools, which cover some of the cost, though many charge students, too: One company, ProctorU, charges students about $15 per test, while another, Proctorio, offers a $100 “lifetime fee.”
 “It’s insanity. I shouldn’t be happy. I know a lot of people aren’t doing so well right now, but for us — I can’t even explain it,” Proctorio’s chief executive Mike Olsen said in an interview. “We’ll probably increase our value by four to five X just this year.”
   The explosive growth casts light on what could be a pivotal moment for mass surveillance in the United States as privacy concerns clash with the unprecedented realities of a modern pandemic. Hundreds of thousands of students have been sent home from universities, and millions of high school students have seen their local schools closed for the rest of the year.
 With more schools pushing to  track students’ locations across campus and their testing behaviors at home, education advocates worry the systems are invading students’ personal lives and reducing the practice of learning to a forensic investigation, where students are presumed cheaters until proven upright.
 “To take a test you need to let a stranger have a video recording of your room? Are you kidding me?” said Bill Fitzgerald, a researcher at the nonprofit group Consumer Reports who specializes in education technology.
   “These platforms exist because they are selling a narrative that students can’t be trusted,” he said. “The people who have the most to lose here are the students, and they’re the farthest away from the decision. … Students are paying tens of thousands of dollars to have their higher-ed institutions sell them out.”
  Students bothered by the system’s intrusive eye previously were given the option of taking their exams the old-fashioned way, in a classroom or a testing center. But with campuses shut down, students’ participation has become effectively mandatory — just before their final exams.
 The systems have already unnerved students like Neil Buettner, a 28-year-old Marine Corps veteran and student at Harford Community College in Churchville, Md., who was incensed by the demands made by the online proctor service Honorlock before taking his microeconomics exam.
   “It’s talking about how it wants to access my computer, my microphone, the webcam. Monitor what’s in the room around me, scan my room. It wants to scan my ID!” he said in an interview. When his professor said he had no option to take the test in person, he opted instead to drop the class. “It’s just a huge step backward,” Buettner said. “Everyone’s giving up their freedom just for the virus.”
 Those concerns have not dented the appeal of companies like Proctorio, which staffs four sales offices in the United States and Europe and oversaw more than 1.2 million students during the December peak. Olsen said he expects their business could more than triple by the school year’s end.
  The company, which typically adds 100 new universities as clients in a single year, is now fielding about 120 leads a day. Big universities that would normally churn through a months-long negotiation now want to rush deals through in a matter of days. And reluctant administrators and professors, he said, are suddenly finding “they’re being forced” to try it out.
   The coronavirus lockdowns have also forced some companies to allow their proctors to work remotely instead of in a supervised office — raising alarms among privacy advocates over who’s gaining access to students’ bedroom video streams. One company, Examity, whose proctor centers in India were recently closed, has posted  job listings for full-time contractors who would start watching test-takers as early as this month.
 The software’s invasive demands on students have also sparked fury among some professors. A faculty group at the University of California at Santa Barbara  wrote a letter to campus leaders last month that argued that the adoption of ProctorU could turn the university into “a surveillance tool.”
 “We recognize … there are trade-offs and unfortunate aspects of the migration online that we must accept,” they wrote. “This is not one of them. We are not willing to sacrifice the privacy and digital rights of our students for the expediency of a take-home final exam.” (A ProctorU attorney responded with a letter threatening legal action over the group’s “defamatory correspondence.”)
   ProctorU’s chief executive, Scott McFarland, said the skeptics are outnumbered by newly interested school leaders: On a single day last month, his office fielded nearly 1,000 calls from educators asking about the service. The company, he said, has worked largely with colleges and private high schools, but the pandemic has opened the possibility of expanding into grade school exams.
 “It was a slow wave, but this changes everything and makes it more like a tsunami event,” he added. “There’s just so much opportunity in places we haven’t really chased before.”
   At the start of a ProctorU test, students are told to show the proctor their student ID cards, their rooms and the tops of their desks to prove they don’t have any cheating material at hand. During the test, the proctor listens through the student’s microphone to ensure he or she does not ask for help from someone out of view.
   The proctor gains access to the test-takers’ computer screens and receives alerts if they do something unacceptable, like copying and pasting text or opening a new browser tab. A video system analyzes the students’ eyes: If they look off-screen for four straight seconds more than two times in a single minute, the motion will be flagged as a suspect event — a hint that they could be referencing notes posted off-screen.
 To ensure the right student is taking the exam, the software uses facial-recognition software to match them to the image on their ID. Random scans are performed throughout the exam to prevent another test-taker from jumping in.
 The company also verifies identities with a typing test: A student may be asked to type 140 words at the beginning of the semester, then again just before testing to verify the speed and rhythms of the student’s keystrokes. Any discrepancies can be flagged for closer inspection.
   A human proctor watches every second of an exam, though the student cannot see the proctor’s face. In previous versions of the software, the student could see the person watching them, but “the creepiness factor always sort of came up,” McFarland said. If a proctor suspects cheating, they alert a more aggressive specialist, known as an “interventionist,” who can demand that the student aim his or her webcam at a suspicious area or face academic penalty.
  Proctors typically work out of one of 11 centers across Alabama, California, India, Jamaica, Panama and the Philippines. But with many of those offices closed, the company said, it is opening backup centers in Canada, hiring more than 100 new workers and instructing many proctors to work from home.
 ProctorU, which oversaw 2 million tests last year from more than 750,000 students, has compiled years of data on students’ 15 “behavioral cheating types,” McFarland said. Students’ tests are live-streamed and recorded for later review: The worst offenders, McFarland said, have had their videos edited together into what he called a cheating “Hall of Fame.”
 ProctorU’s competitors offer similar anti-cheating surveillance with different strategies. Honorlock, a Florida-based company that CEO Michael Hemlepp said has seen “a massive spike in inquiries,” uses software that looks for “attempted dishonesty” and then sends in a human proctor for further review.
 Proctorio goes further, using a completely software-driven approach. After students consent to letting Proctorio monitor their webcams, microphones, desktops or “ any other means necessary to uphold integrity,” the system tracks their speech and eye movements, how long they took to complete the test and how many times they clicked the mouse. It then gives professors an automated report ranking test-takers by “suspicion level” and the number of testing “abnormalities.” Students deemed untrustworthy by the computer are color-coded in red and given an icon of two shadowy figures, reminiscent of the “ Spy vs. Spy” cartoon of Mad magazine fame.
 Chris Dayley, the director of academic testing services at Utah State University, which uses Proctorio, described the software with a laugh as “sort of like spyware that we just legitimize.” And though many students despise the feeling of being watched, Olsen, the company’s chief executive, said the discomfort is worth it if it helps protect the tests. “We’re the police,” he said.
   In an age of social distancing, the companies are racing to show they have the solution to colleges’ testing crisis. Their websites include coronavirus-related advertisements, introductory pricing offers and condensed contracts to,  in Honorlock’s words, “eliminate need for legal intervention and liability concerns.”
 Asim Ali, the executive director of the Biggio Center for the Enhancement of Teaching and Learning at Auburn University, said the school is preparing for a surprise onslaught later this month of more than 200,000 final exams to be overseen by Honorlock and ProctorU as the sprawling Alabama campus empties out for the rest of the spring semester. The systems will cover virtually every test taken by the university’s more than 23,000 undergraduates, whether they had previously consented to the technology or not.
 “It is a crisis situation,” Ali said. “Desperate times call for desperate measures.”
 But some professors and privacy advocates worry the frenzy is leading colleges to approve big corporate data grabs that could leave their students exposed.
  “Students are asked to agree to these decisions, but they have no meaningful power not to consent,” said Guy McHendry, an associate professor at Creighton University, which has used Examity for some proctored exams. “And because we’re doing this with such urgency, we don’t really have time to ingest all the implications of what these companies will do.”
 The companies retain rights to much of what they gather from students’ computers and bedrooms. ProctorU’s  privacy policy for test-takers in California shows the company shares reams of sensitive student data with proctors and schools: their home addresses; details about their work, parental and citizenship status; medical records, including their weight, health conditions and physical or mental disabilities; and biometric data, including fingerprints, facial images, voice recordings and “iris or retina scans.”
 The company said it shares test-takers’ browsing history, searches and online interactions with a group of website analytics providers, which it does not name. The company also said it  retains the right to share all video and audio recordings of the students with their schools to ensure “no exam protocols were violated.” Student data is retained “for as long as necessary,” the  policy states. (McFarland, the company’s chief executive, said it does not sell any information to third parties.)
 Education advocates say they are concerned about how the companies and schools will use this increasingly intimate view of students’ private lives. They also question whether the systems will unfairly penalize students for things that are out of their control: technical glitches, nervous tics or other housemates walking into view. “All of this surveillance,” said Audrey Watters, a writer for the blog Hack Education, “is just not ideal for this very human practice of teaching and learning.”
  On message boards, students claim to have been flagged for stretching to grab a pen or letting their eyes wander during a long exam. One student said on Reddit that their professor had  accused them of cheating during an Honorlock-proctored exam because they had been looking off-screen while working out a math problem by hand.
 On TikTok, students have posted videos about being  relentlessly watched by proctors, “ having a mental breakdown” during an exam, and  crying as the test timer ticks down. Others have shared  ways to cheat (“the hat,” “the yawn”) and  joked about being caught dancing provocatively on camera.
 The mass move to an experimental testing system has left some administrators concerned about what happens next. David Cillay, the vice president for academic outreach at Washington State University, where final exams are scheduled for early May, said he worries about students in rural corners of the state where Internet connections are spotty. “We’re moving into this environment, in the middle of the semester, when we haven’t prepared students to understand all of the technology,” he said.
 But many college leaders say there’s no time for hesitation. Mihran Aroian, a professor at the University of Texas at Austin, said he expects that hundreds of new students will be taking tests for his Foundations of Organizational Behavior and Management class in coming weeks through ProctorU, and that he has no backup plan for students who are uncomfortable with the company’s demands.
 The virus, he said, could mark a “tipping point” for American education. “We can’t assume everything is going to be normal after the end of this,” he added. “It’s a whole new world.”
title: 大学正在使用在线监督服务来监控考试期间的学生。
title_en: Colleges are using online proctoring services to monitor students during exams

note:
Eigengrau(德语意为固有灰色)，亮。自己的灰色[ˈʔaɪ̯gn̩ˌgʁaʊ̯])，也被称为特征(荷兰语和德语中“自己的光”的意思)、暗光或大脑灰色，是许多人报告在没有光线的情况下看到的统一的深灰色背景。本征利希特一词可以追溯到十九世纪[1]，但在最近的科学出版物中很少使用。这种现象的常见科学术语包括视觉噪音或背景改编。这些术语的出现是因为在现象中看到了一个不断变化的小黑白点的领域。[2]。
在正常照明条件下，特征光被认为比黑色物体轻，因为对比度比绝对亮度对视觉系统更重要。[3]例如，由于星星提供的对比度，夜空看起来比艾根格鲁更暗。
研究人员很早就注意到了[什么时候？]。强度-敏感性曲线的形状可以通过假设视网膜中固有的噪声源产生随机事件来解释，这些随机事件与真正的光子触发的事件没有什么区别。[4][5]后来对甘蔗蟾蜍(Bufo Marinus)杆状细胞的实验表明，这些自发事件的频率强烈地依赖于温度，这意味着它们是由视紫红质的热异构化引起的。[6]在人类视杆细胞中，这些事件平均每100秒发生一次，考虑到视紫红质分子在视紫红质细胞中的数量，这意味着视紫红质分子的半衰期约为420年。[7]暗事件和光子反应的不可区分性支持了这一解释，因为视紫红质是转导链的输入。另一方面，不能完全排除神经递质自发释放等过程。[8]。
^Ladd，Trumbull(1894年)。直接控制视网膜视野。“心理学评论”。1(4)：351-55。DOI：10.1037/h0068980。
^Hansen RM，Fulton AB(2000年1月)。背景有轻度早产儿视网膜病变病史的儿童的适应。投资。眼科。维。SCI。41(1)：320-24。PMID为10634637。
^Wallach，Hans(1948)。亮度恒定与消色色的本质。实验心理学杂志。38(3)：310-24。Doi：10.1037/h0053804.。PMID为18865234。
^Barlow，H.B.(1972)。“黑暗与光明的适应：心理物理学”。视觉心理物理学。纽约：斯普林格-维拉格(Springer-Verlag)。ISBN978-0-387-05146-8。
^Barlow，H.B.(1977)。人类视觉中的视网膜和中央因素受到噪音的限制。脊椎动物的光接收功能。纽约：学术出版社。ISBN978-0-12-078950-4。
贝勒，D.A.；马修斯，G；Yau，K.-W.(1980)。蟾蜍视网膜视杆外段电暗噪声的两个分量。生理学杂志。309：591-621。Doi：10.1113/jPhysiol.1980.sp013529.。太平洋投资管理公司上涨了1274605。PMID为6788941。
^Bayeller，Denis A.(1987年1月1日)。光感受器信号与视觉。调查眼科学与视觉科学。28(1)：34-49。PMID为3026986。
罗伯特·沙普利；克里斯蒂娜·恩罗斯-库格尔(1984)。视觉适应和视网膜增益控制。视网膜研究进展。3：263-346。电话：90011-7(84)10.1016/02784327。
note_en:
Eigengrau ( German for "intrinsic gray",  lit. 'own gray';    [ˈʔaɪ̯gn̩ˌgʁaʊ̯]), also called  Eigenlicht ( Dutch and German for "own light"),  dark light, or  brain gray, is the uniform dark  gray background that many people report seeing in the absence of  light. The term  Eigenlicht dates back to the nineteenth century,  [1] but has rarely been used in recent scientific publications. Common scientific terms for the phenomenon include "visual noise" or "background adaptation". These terms arise due to the perception of an ever-changing field of tiny black and white dots seen in the phenomenon.  [2]
 Eigengrau is perceived as lighter than a  black object in normal lighting conditions, because  contrast is more important to the visual system than absolute brightness.  [3] For example, the night sky looks darker than Eigengrau because of the contrast provided by the stars.
   Researchers noticed early on [   when?] that the shape of intensity-sensitivity curves could be explained by assuming that an intrinsic source of noise in the  retina produces random events indistinguishable from those triggered by real  photons.  [4]  [5] Later experiments on  rod cells of  cane toads ( Bufo marinus) showed that the frequency of these spontaneous events is strongly temperature-dependent, which implies that they are caused by the thermal  isomerization of  rhodopsin.  [6] In human rod cells, these events occur about once every 100 seconds on average, which, taking into account the number of rhodopsin molecules in a rod cell, implies that the  half-life of a rhodopsin molecule is about 420 years.  [7] The indistinguishability of dark events from photon responses supports this explanation, because rhodopsin is at the input of the  transduction chain. On the other hand, processes such as the spontaneous release of  neurotransmitters cannot be completely ruled out.  [8]

 ^    Barlow, H.B. (1972). "Dark and Light Adaptation: Psychophysics.".  Visual Psychophysics. New York: Springer-Verlag.  ISBN   978-0-387-05146-8.
 ^    Barlow, H.B. (1977). "Retinal and Central Factors in Human Vision Limited by Noise".    Vertebrate Photoreception. New York: Academic Press.  ISBN   978-0-12-078950-4.
 ^   Baylor, D.A.; Matthews, G; Yau, K.-W. (1980).  "Two components of electrical dark noise in toad retinal rod outer segments".  Journal of Physiology.  309: 591–621.  doi: 10.1113/jphysiol.1980.sp013529.  PMC   1274605.  PMID  6788941.
